title: lepechinia nelsonii
intro: Lepechinia nelsonii, also known as Nelson's mountain balm, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as colds, coughs, and bronchitis, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting immune function. Lepechinia nelsonii can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to soothe sore throats, ease congestion, and promote overall well-being.
